mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces corporate discount rates and borrowing costs, directly compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0 percent, labor market deterioration triggers recession expectations that force immediate equity deleveraging and negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 8.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms robust consumer demand, driving corporate earnings revisions and positive equity momentum.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month contractions in industrial production signal supply-side bottlenecks that compress corporate margins and trigger immediate equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of instances following industrial production contractions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with stagnant policy rates creates a negative real yield environment that erodes purchasing power and forces equity depreciation.
failure_condition: Negative real rate environments yield positive 60-day forward returns in more than 65 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate household income and corporate revenue expectations, generating persistent positive equity momentum.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to correlate with forward returns with a Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth falling below 1.0 percent signals economic stagnation that rapidly translates into negative equity repricing as earnings forecasts are downgraded.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 70 percent of quarters where GDP growth falls below 1.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial output and falling employment indicates productivity-driven expansion that boosts corporate profit margins and equity valuations.
failure_condition: Productivity divergence yields negative forward returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances.
